title: Turkish Charred Flaky Minced Steak Flatbread
description: A date-night turkish dish featuring lean minced steak, topped with a whisk and served on a flaky flatbread. This recipe is anti-inflammatory-friendly and a great option for a healthy meal.
instructions: Combine the minced steak, all-purpose flour, water, olive oil, onion, garlic, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper in a bowl. Mix well until the ingredients are just combined.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 2 equal portions and shape each portion into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 10 inches in diameter.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and cook the flatbread for 1-2 minutes on each side, until it's charred and crispy.
Transfer the flatbread to a baking sheet and top it with the minced steak mixture, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Bake the flatbread in a preheated oven at 425°F (220°C) for 10-12 minutes, until the steak is cooked through and the flatbread is golden brown.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and garnish it with a whisk.
Let the flatbread cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the flatbread into wedges and serve it hot, garnished with additional lemon wedges if desired.
Garnish with chopped fresh herbs like parsley or cilantro if desired.
